ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'croissant' class (FOODON_03315091). No specific class exists for a flattened, caramelized, pistachio-and-chocolate-topped variant. Closest parent match used; gap noted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Croissant — FoodEx2 composite pastry category. Closest defensible anchor for a laminated wheat-flour pastry with added toppings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for pistachio-chocolate flat crisp croissant. A000E (croissant/pastry) used as the closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific pastry variant. SNOMED CT is not used for this record.

preparation_method: caramelized with brown sugar and black sugar
topped with dark chocolate and crushed pistachios

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one croissant piece weighing 80 g, providing roughly 325 kcal, 36.6 g carbohydrate, 16.8 g fat, and 6.6 g protein.

nutrient_source.title: Pistachio & Chocolate Flat Crisp Croissant — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Record G00011003
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record G00011003 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18023 (Croissant, butter) as HPB record does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients set to -1 where not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: Croissant, plain — GI value 67 (analogous estimate); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a pistachio-and-chocolate flat crisp croissant. GI 67 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain croissant (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008, Table entry: Croissant, plain, GI=67, glucose reference). The addition of dark chocolate and pistachios may modestly lower the effective GI due to fat and fibre content, but this effect is minor and the plain-croissant GI is used as the conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 36.64) / 100 ≈ 24.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (300 mg/100 g) combined with high sugar content (11.26 g/100 g) and low potassium (118 mg/100 g), yielding an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this pastry; the 300 mg sodium per 100 g combined with 11.26 g sugar and a sodium-to-potassium ratio exceeding 2.5:1 independently elevates blood pressure risk, and a single 80 g serving delivers 240 mg sodium alongside 9 g sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (45.8 g/100 g) with moderate GI of 67, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 24.6 per 80 g serving, well above the 10-unit threshold for a single food item.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this pastry; the combination of 45.8 g refined carbohydrate per 100 g, a GI of 67, and only 2.6 g dietary fibre produces a high glycemic load (~24.6 per serving) that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 11 g/100 g from butter lamination, combined with 67 mg/100 g cholesterol and 300 mg/100 g sodium, presents a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this pastry; the estimated 11 g saturated fat per 100 g exceeds the AHA single-food saturated fat threshold, and the combined saturated fat, dietary cholesterol, and sodium burden elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk simultaneously.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~11 g/100 g from butter lamination exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold; pistachio and dark chocolate provide minor anti-inflammatory polyphenols insufficient to offset the dominant butter-driven saturated fat profile.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this pastry; the high butter-derived saturated fat content is pro-inflammatory and the beneficial pistachio and dark chocolate components are present in quantities too small to provide a clinically meaningful anti-inflammatory offset.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pistachio and Chocolate Flat Crisp Croissant is a contemporary Singaporean bakery item that reimagines the classic French laminated pastry in a flattened, caramelized format. The croissant dough is pressed flat and coated with a blend of brown sugar and black sugar before baking, which creates a deeply caramelized, lacquered crust with pronounced molasses and toffee notes. The finished pastry is then topped with bittersweet dark chocolate and premium crushed pistachios, delivering a contrasting combination of bitter cocoa richness and the buttery, slightly sweet nuttiness of pistachio. The texture is characteristically crispy and shattering rather than the soft, layered interior of a traditional croissant, making it closer in eating experience to a palmier or kouign-amann. Per 100 g, the pastry provides 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g fat (estimated 11 g saturated from butter lamination), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 67 (medium) based on plain croissant reference data, with a glycemic load of approximately 24.6 per 80 g serving. Clinically, this item is high concern for type 2 diabetes due to its high refined carbohydrate and glycemic load, high concern for hypertension due to elevated sodium and sugar with low potassium, and high concern for cardiovascular disease due to estimated saturated fat and cholesterol content. It is suitable for occasional consumption by healthy adults but should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The item is sold in Singapore bakeries and is popular as a breakfast or snack item.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat pistachio chocolate croissant?
Is this croissant suitable for hypertension?
开心果巧克力可颂热量

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record G00011003 with stated confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy croissant reference as HPB does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions; this introduces minor uncertainty but does not affect the high confidence designation for the primary macros. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain croissant, GI=67), which is the closest defensible approved-source match.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.0 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18023 (Croissant, butter) as the HPB source record G00011003 does not report disaggregated fatty acid fractions. This estimate is consistent with the known butter-laminated composition of this pastry type. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions not reported in HPB Foodo record G00011003 and not available for this specific product in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-17. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in HPB Foodo record G00011003 and detailed values for this specific product are not available in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-17. 4. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on plain croissant data from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). No direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in any approved source. 5. Halal status is listed as false as this product contains butter and eggs from unspecified sources; halal certification status is not confirmed in the HPB record. 6. The input description references '【Mai' which appears to be a truncated brand or vendor name; this has been excluded from the canonical record as it is incomplete and not nutritionally relevant. 7. Cultural classification in the input lists 'Chinese' as primary cuisine; this has been updated to 'Singaporean Bakery / Contemporary Asian Fusion' as the product is a Singapore-market contemporary pastry with French-origin base and Asian flavour profile, sold in Singapore. Multilingual names are provided for all four mandatory Singaporean languages (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) in addition to English. 8. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 entries, as the matched codes represent parent/broader categories rather than exact matches for this specific product variant.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Added saturated_fat_g estimated from USDA SR Legacy #18023 to satisfy Q3. Added all four mandatory Singaporean local-language names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to satisfy G6 and Q1. Added GI value 67 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate (plain croissant) to satisfy Q4. Added health_context for all three required conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with correct human_condition_name for Type 2 Diabetes to satisfy Q7. Expanded natural_language_description to >200 characters to satisfy Q6. Documented all -1 fields in notes to satisfy G3. Corrected cuisine classification from 'Chinese' to 'Singaporean Bakery / Contemporary Asian Fusion'. Verified aliases consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.
Iteration 2: Attempted to correct ontology match_type values; however the string 'broader' was retained in error as it is not in the allowed set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by G5. This resolves the sole remaining hard gate failure identified by the validator in iteration 2.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
